_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how books and simple digital texts are put together — like which way to read and how pages are organised.

_source.w: We are learning how books and simple digital texts are put together — like which way to read and how pages are organised.
y: Understanding how texts are organised helps us become confident readers.
These are the basic rules that all books follow, so we only need to learn them once.
Knowing print concepts means we can focus on the words and stories inside.
eg: We learn that we read English text from left to right and from the top to the bottom.
We find the front cover, the back cover and the title of a book.
We practise turning pages in the right direction.
We notice that a digital text might scroll down instead of having pages to turn.
We point to where the reading starts on a page.
We follow the teacher's pointer as it tracks across a big book during shared reading.
